title: A First Meeting in Hindi
description: Read and answer questions about greetings.

blocks.passage:
📖 Meeting a New Friend

नमस्ते, मेरा नाम सिया है। (Namaste, mera naam Siya hai.) I am learning Hindi one letter at a time. Today I meet a new friend at school. I say, नमस्ते! वह मुस्कुराता है और जवाब देता है, नमस्ते! Then I ask, आपका नाम क्या है? (Aapka naam kya hai?) He says, मेरा नाम आरव है। (Mera naam Aarav hai.)

We look at the Hindi script together. I notice that some letters feel familiar and some feel new. I do not try to read everything at once. Instead, I focus on a few words: नमस्ते, नाम, and धन्यवाद. By the end of the day, I can greet someone politely and introduce myself in Hindi.

questions.q: What is the learner practicing first?
questions.a: The learner is practicing greetings and self-introduction.

questions.q: What is one Hindi word they focus on?
Where do they meet the new friend?
What does the learner do instead of reading everything at once?
questions.a: They focus on a few words at a time.

items.statement: The learner practices one word at a time.
items.explanation: The passage describes slow, step-by-step learning.

items.statement: Hindi is only used in formal books.
items.explanation: Hindi is used in daily conversation too.

items.statement: The learner learns useful everyday language.
items.explanation: The passage focuses on practical words and sentences.

blocks.title: Cultural Note
blocks.content: In Hindi-speaking settings, polite greetings are very common. Using नमस्ते is safe and respectful in most everyday situations.
